metadata.claim_text: Document neurovascular status, key anatomic structures (scapholunate-ligament, lunotriquetral-ligament, scaphoid), stability assessment, and escalation plan for carpal instability.
Carpal Instability evaluation should anchor on mechanism, focused exam, and the anatomic structures most at risk (scapholunate-ligament, lunotriquetral-ligament, scaphoid).
Obtain appropriate hand/wrist imaging for carpal instability and explicitly comment on articular alignment and adjacent joint stability.
Missing scapholunate-ligament assessment in carpal instability is a common board and OITE trap.
Early protected motion and therapy goals for carpal instability should align with tendon, ligament, and joint stability constraints.

metadata.action: Splint/immobilize with therapy plan, repeat exam and imaging, and clear return precautions
metadata.trigger: Stable pattern with acceptable alignment and intact neurovascular exam for carpal instability

metadata.claim_text: Shared hand-wrist anatomy (scapholunate-ligament, lunotriquetral-ligament, scaphoid) links Carpal Instability to adjacent Prepare neighborhoods in the cluster graph.
Treating carpal instability in isolation without checking related wrist, tendon, or nerve neighborhoods in the cluster.
